name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill||lt=kanjō|UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Shizumaro|Q85877756|WD=Q85877756}} name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Village|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Shizumaro|Q85877756|WD=Q85877756}} name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Village|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Shizumaro|Q85877756|WD=Q85877756}} name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Shizumaro|Q85877756|WD=Q85877756}} name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Village|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Shizumaro|Q85877756|WD=Q85877756}} == name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570|1=1554}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930|1=1646}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606|1=1670}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka == name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570|1=1554}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930|1=1646}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606|1=1670}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Village|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka == name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570|1=1554}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930|1=1646}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606|1=1670}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} == name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570|1=1554}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930|1=1646}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606|1=1670}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} == name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570|1=1554}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930|1=1646}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606|1=1670}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in == name in kana (P1814) == * みおやじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5280005005689 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町港町天神原68', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:水祖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876393}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name= Mioya Shrine |Image= [[File:Mioya-jinja (Okinoshima), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location= {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} 68 Tenjinbara, Minatomachi |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity= {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= Unknown |Main hall= |Other name= |Pilgrimage designation= |Annual festival= {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Mioya Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Mioya Shrine''' (みおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tenjinbara, Minatomachi, {{ill|lt=Okinoshima Town|Q1205039|WD=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|WD=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The shrine enshrines the following two deitiesExplanation plaque on the shrine grounds.. ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Associated deity :* {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} This shrine is identified with the “Mioya Shrine” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but this identification dates only from after the late Edo-period work *Oki Kokishū*. Medieval and early modern sources instead describe it as a shrine dedicated to {{ill|lt=Tenjin faith|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|lt=Tenman Tenjin|1=Tenjin faith}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. This is understood to be because, at some point, Tenman Tenjin was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kanjō|1=UNKNOWN}} at Mioya Shrine, and as devotion to Tenman Tenjin increased, it became better known than the original Mioya Shrine deity {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The associated deity Sugawara no Michizane corresponds to this Tenman Tenjin, and even today Mioya Shrine itself is commonly called “Tenjin-san” {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. There is another shrine of the same name, Mioya Shrine, in Hatta Enokihara, Okinoshima Town; according to tradition, the deity of that shrine is said to have drifted to become the Mioya Shrine at Tenjinbara“Gokokuji Village,” *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 33: Place Names of Shimane Prefecture*. Heibonsha, 1995.. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}. The hill behind the shrine buildings is also said to be an {{ill|lt=ancient burial mound|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), “Mioya Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|WD=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} {{ill|lt=Shūki District|Q10920245|WD=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, and this shrine is identified with that entry {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. The *Oki Jinmyōchō* also records “Junior Fourth Rank, Mioya Myōjin,” but details of changes during the medieval period are unknown {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}{{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. A ridge tag from {{ill|lt=Tenmon (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} 22 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570|1=1554}}) refers to “Tenman Daijizai Tenjin,” and ridge tags from {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1646|Q6930|WD=Q6930|1=1646}}) and {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1670|Q7606|WD=Q7606|1=1670}}) also describe it as a shrine dedicated to Tenman Tenjin {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. In addition, the *Inshū Shichō Gōki* from {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}}) and the *Inshūki* from {{ill|lt=Jōkyō|Q1146221|WD=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}}) both record it as a Tenmangū {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. Later, beginning with the late Edo-period *Oki Kokishū*, which records the name “Gōso Shrine,” various works thereafter note that it should be identified with the “Mioya Shrine” of the *Engishiki* register {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was fixed as “Mioya Shrine,” and in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{Sfn|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fourth Rank (*Oki Jinmyōchō*) — recorded as “Mioya Myōjin.” === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === References === * Explanation plaque on the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Compiled by the Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Records|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/962}} *Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 2*] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), see frame 962. * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Shikinaisha Research Association|author=Handa Yaichirō|chapter=Mioya Shrine|year=1983|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=Yao Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490336|ref={{Harvid|Yao Village (Heibonsha)|1995}}}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor={{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Ishizuka Takatoshi|year=2000|chapter=Mioya Shrine|title=Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 7, San’in |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=978-4560025079|ref={{Harvid|Mioya Shrine (Kami)|2000}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka
